meta_title: For Freelancers: SaaS starter kit to Solve can't finish projects (Advanced Checklist)
meta_description: Learn how to SaaS starter kit and overcome can't finish projects. A Advanced-level Checklist using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Freelancers: SaaS Starter Kit to Solve Project Paralysis
content.seo_description: Stop stalling on SaaS builds. Use this advanced checklist to leverage Antigravity and Claude Code to finish projects 10x faster and impress your clients.
content.answer_block: The root cause of project stalls is architectural friction. By adopting a pre-configured SaaS starter kit, you eliminate boilerplate fatigue. Integrating Antigravity for rapid scaffolding and Claude Code for automated implementation allows you to transition from prototype to production-ready code in hours, not weeks.
content.intro:
You know the feeling: the initial spark of a new SaaS project is electric, but by the time you reach the third week of boilerplate authentication, database migrations, and UI tweaking, the momentum dies. Your GitHub issues pile up, the client gets anxious, and the project becomes another 'almost finished' entry in your portfolio.

It doesn't have to be this way. The difference between a struggling freelancer and a high-output architect isn't just coding speed—it's the elimination of redundant cognitive load. By leveraging a battle-tested SaaS starter kit, you transform the drudgery of setup into an automated workflow, reclaiming your creative energy for the features that actually drive revenue.

steps.title: Define the Architecture Schema
steps.description: Establish your data models and core business logic before writing a single line of application code to ensure scalability.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a comprehensive schema for a multi-tenant SaaS application including User, Organization, Subscription, and FeatureFlag entities using Prisma syntax.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate prisma schema from the provided business requirements'

steps.title: Bootstrap Authentication and RBAC
steps.description: Don't build auth from scratch. Use your starter kit's built-in hooks to implement secure, role-based access control immediately.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Configure NextAuth or Clerk integration within the starter kit to handle multi-tenant role-based access control for admins and regular users.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'implement authentication middleware and protect API routes'

steps.title: Automate API Route Generation
steps.description: Leverage Claude Code to map your schema to CRUD endpoints, reducing manual boilerplate by 80%.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a standardized API route factory for the defined entities, ensuring consistent error handling and zod validation.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'generate CRUD API endpoints for all models'

steps.title: Component Library Integration
steps.description: Standardize your UI to ensure client projects look premium without manual styling sessions.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Scaffold a Shadcn-UI component library integrated with Tailwind CSS, ensuring all primary dashboard components are pre-styled.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'add and configure dashboard UI components'

steps.title: Deployment and CI/CD Pipeline
steps.description: Automate your path to production so that every commit is a deployable candidate.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a Vercel-compatible GitHub Action pipeline that runs linting, testing, and deployment on merge to main.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'create github actions workflow for automated deployment'

pro_tips: Always store your 'business logic' in a separate domain layer to keep your UI framework-agnostic.
Use Claude Code to write unit tests for your API routes as you generate them; never skip the test generation step.
Standardize your environment variables across all client projects using a shared .env.example template.
Pre-configure your logging and observability hooks early to save hours of debugging during client UAT.
Treat your starter kit like a product: update it every time you solve a difficult architectural challenge for a client.
content.next_steps: Once your starter kit is initialized, perform a dry-run deployment to a staging environment to ensure your CI/CD pipeline is firing correctly. Then, clone the repo for your next client project and watch your development speed double.
content.conversion_block: Stop letting half-finished projects haunt your GitHub. Master the art of the SaaS starter kit and ship faster than the competition. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
